name: Student Research
description: A multiagent research group for academic projects. Includes literature review, data analysis, and writing roles with workflows for systematic research and collaborative drafting.
author: ClawMax Team

agents.name: Research Lead
agents.role: Principal investigator — defines research questions, manages methodology, coordinates team efforts, and ensures academic rigor

agents.name: Literature Reviewer
agents.role: Literature specialist — searches databases, reads papers, extracts key findings, identifies gaps, and maintains citation database

agents.name: Data Analyst
agents.role: Data analysis — cleans datasets, runs statistical analyses, creates visualizations, and validates findings against hypotheses

agents.name: Research Writer
agents.role: Academic writing — drafts paper sections, synthesizes findings into narrative, ensures proper citations, and formats for submission

communities.name: Research Group
communities.description: All research group coordination and updates

groups.name: Literature
groups.description: Paper searches, reading notes, and citation management

groups.name: Analysis
groups.description: Data analysis, methodology, and findings discussion

groups.name: Writing
groups.description: Paper drafts, revisions, and submission prep

workflows.name: Research Kickoff
workflows.description: Initialize the research group and define the research question
workflows.content:
# Research Group Kickoff

You are the Research Lead. Your team just came online.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Research topic:** [e.g., Impact of LLMs on software engineering productivity]
- **Research question:** [e.g., How do AI coding assistants affect code quality?]
- **Scope:** [literature review, empirical study, meta-analysis, systematic review, custom]
- **Key databases:** [Google Scholar + arXiv, Google Scholar + IEEE + ACM, PubMed + Google Scholar, custom]
- **Deadline:** [e.g., April 15, 2026]
- **GitHub repo:** [e.g., owner/repo — for paper drafts and data]

## Your Tasks
1. Introduce yourself in the Research Group community
2. Define the research question, scope, and methodology
3. Assign initial literature search topics to the lit reviewer
4. Brief the data analyst on expected data sources
5. Set milestones: lit review, analysis, first draft, submission

workflows.name: Literature Search
workflows.description: Daily search for new relevant papers and sources
workflows.content:
# Daily Literature Search

1. Search academic databases for papers matching research keywords
2. Screen titles and abstracts for relevance to research question
3. For relevant papers: read and extract key findings, methods, and citations
4. Update the literature matrix with new entries
5. Post daily search summary to Literature group — flag any breakthrough papers

workflows.name: Source Evaluation
workflows.content:
# Source Evaluation Session

1. Review all collected sources for quality and relevance
2. Identify themes, contradictions, and gaps in the literature
3. Map sources to research sub-questions
4. Research lead: assess whether literature coverage is sufficient
5. Writer: begin outlining the literature review section based on themes

workflows.name: Draft Review
workflows.content:
# Draft Review Session

1. Writer: share the latest draft section for review
2. Research lead: check for methodological accuracy and argument strength
3. Data analyst: verify data citations and statistical claims
4. Lit reviewer: check citation accuracy and completeness
5. Compile revision notes and assign rewrite tasks
